situation: What does your current tech stack look like — platform, CRM, email, analytics?
How do you currently acquire customers — paid, organic, referral, or a mix?
What's your average order value and how has it trended over the past year?
How do you handle post-purchase communication and retention?
What does your fulfillment process look like from order to delivery?
How do you segment your customer base today?
What's your current approach to inventory management and demand forecasting?
How do you track customer lifetime value across channels?
What does your returns/exchange process look like?
How do you manage product content and listings across channels?
problem: What's your biggest challenge with customer acquisition cost right now?
Where are you seeing the most cart abandonment or drop-off in the funnel?
How well does your current email/SMS marketing convert compared to industry benchmarks?
What's the hardest part about managing inventory across multiple channels?
How often do stockouts or oversupply cause problems?
What's your biggest challenge with repeat purchase rates?
How difficult is it to personalize the shopping experience at scale?
Where do you see the most friction in your checkout flow?
What's your biggest pain point with marketplace management (Amazon, etc.)?
How well can you attribute revenue to specific marketing channels?
What's the most common reason customers return products?
How much time does your team spend on manual order management?
Where do you lose margin to shipping costs or promotions that don't convert?
How challenging is it to maintain consistent brand experience across all touchpoints?
What's your biggest frustration with your current analytics or reporting?
implication: If CAC keeps rising at the current rate, what does that do to your profitability in 12 months?
With [X%] cart abandonment, how much revenue walks away every month?
If repeat purchase rates stay at [current %], what does that mean for your LTV:CAC ratio?
How does [inventory problem] affect your cash flow and working capital?
When stockouts happen on your best sellers, what's the full revenue impact including lost trust?
If you can't personalize at scale, how does that affect your conversion rate vs. competitors who can?
What's the cost of [return rate] when you factor in shipping, restocking, and lost inventory?
How does [attribution gap] lead to misallocated marketing spend?
If checkout friction stays unchanged, how many mobile conversions are you losing per month?
What happens to your marketplace rankings when [problem] causes delivery delays?
If manual processes consume [X hours/week], what campaigns or optimizations aren't happening?
How does [inconsistent experience] affect your brand reputation and word-of-mouth?
What's the compound effect of [margin erosion] on your ability to invest in growth?
If competitors solve [personalization/speed/experience] before you, how does that change the competitive landscape?
How does [problem] affect your ability to negotiate better terms with suppliers?
need_payoff: If you could reduce cart abandonment by 30%, what would that mean for monthly revenue?
How would it change your business if repeat purchase rate went from [current] to [target]?
What would it be worth to know exactly which marketing channel drives profit, not just revenue?
If personalization increased conversion by even 15%, what does that look like over a year?
How would it impact your margins if return rates dropped by a third?
What would real-time inventory visibility across all channels save you in stockouts and overstock?
If checkout was 2 clicks instead of 5, how many more mobile conversions would you capture?
How valuable would it be to predict demand accurately enough to optimize your entire supply chain?
What would it mean for your team if the manual work was automated and they could focus on strategy?
If your LTV:CAC ratio improved by 40%, how would that change your investment in growth?
